video_title: CFTC Chairman Mike Selig: Crypto Perps Are Coming to America
gist: CFTC Chairman Mike Selig confirms the US has opened the door to onshore crypto perpetual futures, starting with a Bitcoin perp approval for Kalshi and a Coinbase no-action letter to access Deribit, with 17+ additional digital commodity perps already self-certified in the first week. The regulatory framework tiers crypto assets (digital commodities auto-certifiable, meme coins/collectibles require full approval, equities need joint CFTC-SEC process), caps leverage at 5-10x versus 250x+ offshore, and explicitly aims to pull all offshore perp volume — including pre-IPO stock perps — into a US-regulated structure.

themes.theme: US Perp Market Opening — Scale and Speed

insights.claim: CFTC approved the first onshore US Bitcoin perpetual futures contract listed on Kalshi's exchange, and simultaneously issued Coinbase a no-action letter allowing it to route customers to Deribit for futures, options, and perps — two simultaneous landmark actions in a single week.
insights.speaker: Ryan Sean Adams (host) / Mike Selig
insights.so_what: This is the regulatory starting gun; exchanges that can move fast to list CFTC-compliant perps gain first-mover advantage in a market that was entirely offshore.
insights.quote: the CFTC announced the order for approval to Koshi X for the listing of the BTC per contract... also the first onshore US Bitcoin perpetual futures contract from a CFTC registered exchange

insights.claim: Within one week of the Bitcoin perp approval, approximately 17 additional digital commodity assets beyond Bitcoin were self-certified or in the process of being self-certified as perpetual contracts by exchanges.
insights.speaker: Mike Selig
insights.so_what: The velocity of self-certifications suggests a wave of new onshore perp products is imminent across major L1 tokens; platforms with CFTC registration are racing to list.
insights.quote: there's about 17 other assets beyond Bitcoin that we saw self-certified by exchanges are in the process of being self-certified in the past week

insights.claim: The CFTC has not approved a new type of derivative instrument for over a decade; perpetual futures represent the first new derivative category in more than 10 years, and Selig calls it just the beginning.
insights.speaker: Mike Selig
insights.so_what: Structural scarcity of new derivative types means this approval sets a precedent that will be cited for years; exchanges that establish early market share in perps own a structurally defensible position.
insights.quote: The CFTC hasn't approved a new type of derivative instrument for over a decade. I I think it's incredibly exciting that we now have perpetuals in the lineup

insights.claim: Coinbase, Robinhood, Kraken, OKX, Kalshi, Polymarket, Hyperliquid, Lighter, Bitnomial, and Gemini are all actively working with or in discussions with the CFTC to offer US perp products — signaling a gold rush for market share.
insights.speaker: Ryan Sean Adams (host) / Mike Selig
insights.so_what: The competitive field is large and includes CeFi giants, prediction market platforms, and DeFi protocols — incumbents and disruptors will compete for the same US perp volume.
insights.quote: We're working with virtually all these platforms.

themes.theme: Regulatory Tiering Framework for Crypto Assets

insights.claim: CFTC Chair Selig and SEC Chair Atkins jointly created a crypto asset categorization framework — digital commodities (Bitcoin, ETH, SOL, UNI), digital collectibles/meme coins, digital tools (liquid staking receipts), digital securities (tokenized stocks), and stablecoins — that determines the regulatory pathway for perp listing without requiring the Clarity Act to pass.
insights.speaker: Mike Selig
insights.so_what: This executive-level interpretation sidesteps pending legislation; assets classified as digital commodities are already perp-eligible via self-certification, making the regulatory pathway clearer now than markets previously assumed.
insights.quote: Chairman Atkins and I came together, worked together on an interpretation that divided up crypto assets into categories

insights.claim: Digital commodity perps (Bitcoin, Ethereum, Solana, Uniswap-type tokens) can be self-certified by CFTC-registered exchanges without prior approval — the exchange submits the contract and the CFTC can reject it, but listing can proceed immediately, creating a presumption of permissibility.
insights.speaker: Mike Selig
insights.so_what: Self-certification eliminates the bottleneck of one-by-one CFTC approval; the practical implication is that major L1 perps can launch on US exchanges in weeks, not years.
insights.quote: the exchange can list those products without having to ask for permission first. They can essentially submit them and we can reject the contract if we believe there's an issue

insights.claim: Meme coins and digital collectibles require a full staff-level approval process at the CFTC before perp listing, while equities and metals perps require engagement with both CFTC and SEC staff — creating a clear three-tier speed hierarchy (fast/medium/slow) for perp listings.
insights.speaker: Mike Selig
insights.so_what: Meme coin perps and equity perps face meaningfully longer timelines than L1 crypto perps; investors should not expect PEPE or pre-IPO stock perps onshore imminently despite market excitement.
insights.quote: digital uh collectibles for example, meme coins, those sorts of assets, we would expect a process with the staff. We want those to go through a full approval with the CFTC

themes.theme: Structural Differences: US vs. Offshore Perps

insights.claim: US onshore perps will be capped at approximately 5-10x leverage versus 250x or higher offshore, based on CFTC regulations and clearing house parameters tied to trailing 30-day price averages — a fundamental product difference that may limit retail speculator adoption but should attract institutional capital.
insights.speaker: Mike Selig
insights.so_what: Lower leverage reduces open interest as a percentage of notional but de-risks liquidation cascades; US perp platforms will likely attract institutional flow while retail leverage-seekers remain offshore.
insights.quote: in the United States we've seen more 5 to 7x range sometimes getting up to 10... nothing like you're getting offshore

insights.claim: All US perps must clear through a central counterparty clearing house (CCP), with broker intermediation common — a structural safeguard that contrasts with direct smart-contract settlement in offshore/onchain perps and provides counterparty default protection.
insights.speaker: Mike Selig
insights.so_what: CCP clearing means US perps carry near-zero counterparty risk versus exchange/protocol failure risk offshore; this is the key selling point for institutional adoption and positions US-regulated venues favorably post-FTX.
insights.quote: each contract is run through a central counterparty clearing house... there are certain controls that protect customer funds

insights.claim: Selig explicitly cited FTX as a cautionary tale and noted that the CFTC-regulated FTX entity was the only part of FTX that protected customer funds, using this as evidence that US regulation works — a direct argument that regulatory compliance is a feature, not a burden, for exchanges.
insights.speaker: Mike Selig
insights.so_what: Exchanges that obtain CFTC registration can credibly market themselves as the safe, compliant alternative to offshore venues; this narrative supports premium pricing and institutional trust.
insights.quote: the CFTC regulated FTX entity was the only one that was safe that that protected customer funds

themes.theme: Real World Asset and Equity Perps — The Bigger Opportunity

insights.claim: Real-world asset (RWA) perps — covering oil, gold, and pre-IPO stocks including Anthropic, SpaceX, and OpenAI — now represent over 60% of total perp market volume offshore, surpassing crypto perps, yet these markets are entirely hosted offshore despite covering American companies.
insights.speaker: Ryan Sean Adams (host)
insights.so_what: RWA perps are already the dominant use case by volume; bringing these onshore is a larger regulatory and commercial prize than crypto perps alone — watch for CFTC-SEC joint action on security-based derivatives.
insights.quote: real world asset pers are actually dominating volume over cryptos. Per I think it's something over over 60% of the volume of the per market uh is now looking at real world assets like oil, gold and now also these preipo stocks

insights.claim: Equity perpetuals (security-based derivatives) require joint CFTC and SEC authorization under existing statute — the exchange registers with CFTC and then undergoes an abbreviated registration with the SEC — a precedent that already exists for security futures but has historically stalled due to CFTC-SEC turf battles.
insights.speaker: Mike Selig
insights.so_what: The CFTC-SEC coordination under Selig and Atkins is the unlock for equity perps; if the two chairs continue cooperating, pre-IPO and equity perp markets could move onshore faster than consensus expects.
insights.quote: security futures, so whether it's a perpetual or not, those products require joint uh authorization by both agencies

insights.claim: Selig explicitly ruled out agricultural commodity perps (corn, live cattle) in the near term, citing seasonal delivery mechanics and lack of industry demand — focusing CFTC's perp ambitions on crypto digital commodities and equity products only.
insights.speaker: Mike Selig
insights.so_what: CME Group and agricultural incumbent exchanges face no near-term perp competition threat in their core commodity contracts; the incumbents' moats in grains and livestock are not challenged by this regulatory shift.
insights.quote: We don't necessarily think that people uh that there's a place for corn perpetual contracts. We actually haven't gotten requests for that.

themes.theme: Onchain/DeFi Perps and the Regulatory Path for Decentralized Venues

insights.claim: CFTC is actively engaging with onchain perp protocol developers (including implicitly Hyperliquid and Lighter) to create a tailored regulatory pathway — explicitly rejecting the prior administration's approach of requiring DeFi protocols to register as if they were 1934-era exchanges.
insights.speaker: Mike Selig
insights.so_what: DeFi perp protocols face a more accommodating regulatory environment than under Gensler/Behnam — but the path requires KYC-compatible architecture, which is structurally incompatible with fully permissionless protocols like Hyperliquid.
insights.quote: We're leaning into the future. We understand that our markets are changing, that technology is changing. We want to tailor fit our rules and regulations for these new products and platforms

insights.claim: CFTC has issued guidance permitting self-custodial wallet providers to offer exchange access without registering as brokers, and is exploring allowing onchain assets to be used for settlement of futures contracts — two specific rule changes that reduce friction for DeFi integration.
insights.speaker: Mike Selig
insights.so_what: These two policy moves directly lower the regulatory burden for DeFi-adjacent perp infrastructure; wallet providers and onchain settlement rails benefit specifically.
insights.quote: We've been taking a number of measures at the commission to to make sure that self-custodial wallet providers, for example, are able to offer access without having to register as a broker

insights.claim: Hyperliquid's KYC-incompatible architecture is an explicit structural barrier to CFTC compliance, though Selig acknowledged the platform's popularity (including a mention of Hunter Biden using it) — suggesting regulatory tolerance for offshore DeFi platforms while prioritizing onshore alternatives.
insights.speaker: Mike Selig / Ryan Sean Adams
insights.so_what: Hyperliquid and similar permissionless perp DEXes will likely remain offshore-dominant; CFTC's perp push benefits CeFi-hybrid platforms that can implement KYC while preserving onchain transparency.
insights.quote: I don't think Hyperlid will ever mandate or enforce KYC is just kind of antagonistic to their to their platform

insights.claim: CFTC has greenlighted 24/7 trading for crypto perp exchanges, formalizing what has been informal practice and positioning blockchain-native continuous markets as the template for future derivatives market structure.
insights.speaker: Mike Selig
insights.so_what: 24/7 CFTC guidance removes a structural ambiguity for US-registered exchanges; it validates that perp platforms can operate around the clock without compliance risk, enabling direct competition with offshore venues on availability.
insights.quote: we just put out some guidance on 24/7 because exchanges are ready for it. They're excited about it.

insights.claim: CFTC views blockchain's transparency as a long-run substitute for traditional trade reporting to data repositories — anticipating that onchain transaction data can replace regulatory reporting obligations once analytics tools mature, reducing compliance cost for onchain venues.
insights.speaker: Mike Selig
insights.so_what: If CFTC follows through, onchain perp platforms could eventually face lower ongoing compliance costs than CeFi counterparts; this is a long-term structural advantage for DeFi if analytics tools close the gap on bad-actor identification.
insights.quote: I do expect as uh onchain technology continues to advance, it will become much easier for regulators to really lean into the technology... replace things like trade reporting to data repositories with onchain transaction data

themes.theme: Market Structure and Systemic Risk Considerations

insights.claim: Crypto derivatives volume is now approximately 90% perp / 10% spot — a ratio that Selig argues is comparable to other commodity markets globally where futures vastly dwarf spot, and not necessarily a systemic risk signal unique to crypto.
insights.speaker: Mike Selig
insights.so_what: Selig's framing deflates the 'crypto is over-leveraged' narrative by contextualizing it against global derivatives norms; this matters for regulatory risk pricing — the CFTC is unlikely to restrict perp growth on systemic grounds alone.
insights.quote: if you look at other commodity asset classes, this is not uncommon because you have so much leverage in the derivatives markets. So our global derivatives markets are actually 1.2 quadrillion notional

insights.claim: CFTC's manipulation-susceptibility test — comparing the depth of spot markets relative to futures open interest — will be the key gating criterion for which assets qualify for perp listing, implicitly disadvantaging small-cap and low-liquidity tokens regardless of their asset category.
insights.speaker: Mike Selig
insights.so_what: Small-cap crypto tokens with thin spot markets will fail the manipulation test even if categorized as digital commodities; investors in illiquid altcoins should not expect perp listings to provide a near-term price catalyst.
insights.quote: if we're looking at, for example, Bitcoin relative to, you know, some meme coin of choice, Pepe or something like that... we're going to think about how much uh supply there is, how much spot trading, how much uh susceptibility there is to manipulation

insights.claim: CFTC's mandate is fair competition, not protection of incumbents — Selig explicitly stated that CME Group and other legacy commodity exchanges are welcome to offer perps and will face direct competition from crypto-native and onchain entrants on the same regulatory playing field.
insights.speaker: Mike Selig
insights.so_what: CME Group's dominant position in Bitcoin futures faces structural competition risk from perp-focused entrants; CME's 24-hour but not 24/7 model and dated contract structure are disadvantages vs. perpetuals in the same regulatory tier.
insights.quote: our mandate as an agency is fair competition. So, we're not standing in the way of any incumbents from coming into these markets and offering new products

insights.claim: Kraken, which owns Bitnomial (which already has a perpetual-style contract), and Gemini have both made social media announcements indicating perp product plans — positioning them as the next wave of US perp launchers after Kalshi and Coinbase.
insights.speaker: Mike Selig
insights.so_what: Kraken's ownership of Bitnomial gives it a structural head start on CFTC registration for perps; watch for Kraken/Bitnomial and Gemini as the next material US perp product announcements.
insights.quote: I believe Kraken and Gemini, which Kraken Kraken actually owns Bitnomial, believe they've made announcements on social media
